commit.message:
Add future posts queue system with daily automation

New files:
- data/future-posts.json: Queue of pending posts to generate
- scripts/get_next_post.py: Gets next post from queue and marks completed
- scripts/daily_post_generator.sh: Daily script that auto-generates posts
- systemd/arabfalak-daily-post.service: Systemd service for daily posts
- systemd/arabfalak-daily-post.timer: Timer runs at 2 AM daily

Features:
✅ Manual control via future-posts.json
✅ Automatic daily post generation at 2 AM
✅ Queue tracks pending and completed posts
✅ Auto-deploys to GitHub Pages after generation
✅ Category can be customized per post

Usage:
- Edit data/future-posts.json to add/remove posts
- Posts move from 'pending' to 'completed' automatically
- Check logs: journalctl -u arabfalak-daily-post.service -f

  Balance, Candlestick, Event, Series

* feat: add Kalshi RSA-PSS authentication utilities

Implement sign_request() and get_websocket_auth_headers() functions that
produce the three KALSHI-ACCESS-* headers required by the Kalshi REST
and WebSocket APIs.  Uses the cryptography library for RSA-PSS signing
with SHA-256 and maximum salt length.

* Add async Kalshi REST API client with retry logic and request signing

Implements KalshiRestClient wrapping all Kalshi v2 REST endpoints:
- Market discovery (markets, events, series, orderbook, candlesticks, trades)
- Portfolio management (balance, positions, orders, fills)
- Order actions (create, cancel)
- Exchange info (status, schedule, account limits)
- Historical data (cutoff, markets, fills)

Features:
- httpx.AsyncClient with async context manager support
- Automatic RSA-PSS request signing via backend.utils.auth
- Retry with exponential backoff + jitter for 429 and 5xx errors
- Structured logging via structlog (no key material logged)

- Subscribe/unsubscribe/list/update subscription commands
- All 7 channels: ticker, orderbook_delta, trade, market_lifecycle_v2,
  fill, user_orders, market_positions
- Exponential backoff reconnection with subscription replay
- Per-subscription sequence tracking for orderbook integrity

commit.message:
test(w72): proptest invariant expansion across 4 crates (~50 tests) (#606)

## Summary

Adds **53 property-based invariant tests** across 4 crates using the
\proptest\ framework.

### tokmd-model (15 tests)
- Total code lines == sum of per-language code lines
- Module aggregation preserves total counts
- File count consistency and sorting invariants
- \vg()\ edge cases, \
ormalize_path\ properties, \module_key\ determinism

### tokmd-scan (10 tests)
- Scan idempotency (code counts and line counts)
- Excluding \**/*\ produces zero counts
- Adding excludes never increases code counts
- Arbitrary \ScanOptions\ never panic
- Code lines <= total lines, nonexistent path errors
- Hidden and doc-string flags preserve invariants

### tokmd-gate (15 tests)
- \q(x,x)\ passes, \
e(x,x)\ fails, \gt(x,x)\ fails, \gte(x,x)\ passes
- \lt(x,x)\ fails, \lte(x,x)\ passes (reflexive/irreflexive operators)
- Gate evaluation determinism, negate inversion
- Exists on present/absent keys, pointer root resolution
- Ratchet identity and large-increase invariants

### tokmd-badge (13 tests)
- SVG structure validity (opens/closes, xmlns, 2 rects, 2 texts)
- Width positive, height == 24, label/value presence
- XML escaping, determinism, width scaling, minimum width 120px

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(w72): mutation-hardening tests for math + redact + path (~45 tests) (#603)

## Summary

Add mutation-hardening integration tests targeting common
mutation-testing survivor patterns across three crates.

### Test coverage

| Crate | Tests | Targets |
|-------|-------|---------|
| \ okmd-math\ | 20 | \ ound_f64\ boundaries, \safe_ratio\ div-by-zero,
\percentile\ edge cases, \gini_coefficient\ inequality ordering |
| \ okmd-redact\ | 15 | Hash length/determinism, separator
normalization, dot-segment cleaning, redact never returns original |
| \ okmd-path\ | 15 | Backslash conversion, empty strings, idempotency,
\../\ preservation, consecutive separators |

### Mutation patterns targeted
- **Boundary conditions**: exact .5 rounding, 0th/100th percentile,
empty slices
- **Boolean-logic flips**: \== 0\ guard in safe_ratio, \is_empty()\
checks
- **Comparison-operator swaps**: Gini monotonicity ordering, inequality
direction
- **Off-by-one errors**: percentile index computation, hash truncation
length
- **Constant replacement**: zero-return paths, identity ratios

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix platform color type in GenerateDifferenceMask and GenerateDifferenceImage

Use SKColorType.Bgra8888 explicitly for diff bitmaps, matching the
format used by GetNormalizedBitmap. The default SKImageInfo uses
PlatformColorType which is RGBA on macOS, causing channel swaps

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Return diff images in platform color type, add encode roundtrip tests

GetPixelSpan<SKColor>() is a raw memory blit with no color-type
swizzle. SKColor's ARGB uint matches BGRA8888 byte layout on LE,
so raw span writes only produce correct pixels in BGRA8888 buffers.
On RGBA8888 platforms (e.g. macOS), writing SKColor to a platform-
default bitmap via raw span swaps R and B in the encoded output.

Fix: build diff in BGRA8888 for correct span writes, then canvas-
draw to a platform-default bitmap before returning. This gives
callers an image in the expected platform layout that encodes and
displays correctly on all platforms.

Add SKColorLayoutTest with encode-roundtrip tests that verify the
diff image channels survive PNG encode/decode regardless of platform.

commit.message:
fix(codegen): fix WithOverflow secondary remapping and add 128-bit overflow decomposition

In copy_inst, all six \*WithOverflow operations were silently dropping
their secondary (overflow flag) result during legalization, so any
instruction referencing secondary_of_overflow_op would see an
unmapped ValueRef and produce undefined behavior.

Fix by capturing the secondary result and recording it in the vmap
for the six non-128-bit cases (SAdd/UAdd/SSub/USub/SMul/UMulWithOverflow
with bits <= 64), mirroring the existing LoadAtomic/AtomicRmw pattern.

For the 128-bit cases (bits > 64), the old pass-through left the
operands remapped only to their lo halves, producing wrong arithmetic.
Add dedicated decomposition functions:
- leg_sadd/uadd_with_overflow_128: compute (lo, hi) + overflow flag
- leg_ssub/usub_with_overflow_128: same for subtraction
- leg_smul/umul_with_overflow_128: product via leg_mul; overflow=false

Signed-overflow detection uses the XOR-based formula:
  ((a_hi ^ result_hi) & (b_hi ^ result_hi)) < 0 (signed)
Unsigned-overflow detection uses double-carry checks on hi.
